info.title: Idea Campaign RPC (v1)
info.description:
JSON-RPC interface for the public idea campaign host surface, currently implemented by the TS idea-engine runtime. Contract schemas are referenced via $ref to sibling files in this directory.

Scope note: the search/eval runtime has been archived. The engine manages an idea portfolio: campaign lifecycle, node reads, externally computed belief posteriors (node.set_posterior), lifecycle transitions (node.set_lifecycle), posterior-based ranking (rank.compute), and promotion (node.promote).

Ref resolution: All external schema $ref values are relative to this OpenRPC document's directory.

Idempotency semantics (MUST):
1) Side-effecting methods require idempotency_key.
2) Engine deduplicates by (method, campaign_id, idempotency_key). For campaign.init (no campaign_id yet), dedupe by (method, idempotency_key).
2b) If the same idempotency_key is reused with a different request payload, the engine MUST reject with schema_validation_failed (-32002) and MUST NOT execute the request. The error MUST include error.data.reason="idempotency_key_conflict" and MUST include error.data.idempotency_key + error.data.payload_hash.
2c) Payload identity MUST be machine-defined: compute payload_hash = sha256(JCS(params_without_idempotency_key)). JCS = RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme (deterministic object key order + number formatting). Engines SHOULD apply default-value filling (e.g., node.list.limit omitted → 50) before hashing to avoid false conflicts for semantically identical intents.
3) On duplicate, engine MUST return the same logical response as the first call (result or error), without repeating side-effects. The response MUST include idempotency metadata (idempotency_key + is_replay); is_replay MUST be true on duplicate hits and false on first execution. All other fields MUST match the first response. For non-deterministic methods, this MUST be implemented by storing and replaying the first response, not by re-execution.
4) Idempotency records MUST be retained for the campaign lifetime (or until an explicit campaign.delete is added).

Error data contract (MUST):
All error responses MUST include a machine-readable JSON-RPC error.data object. Minimum fields: {"reason": string, "details"?: object}.
- For code -32001 (budget_exhausted), error.data.reason MUST be "dimension_exhausted" and error.data.details.exhausted_dimensions MUST list one or more exhausted budget dimensions.
- For code -32002 (schema_validation_failed), error.data.reason MUST distinguish at least: schema_invalid, idempotency_key_conflict, activation_condition_required, activation_condition_unexpected.
- For code -32017 (promotion_blocked), error.data.reason MUST be posterior_missing or node_not_active.

params.name: abstract_problem_registry
params.description: Optional caller-provided abstract problem registry. There is no engine built-in registry. The engine MUST reject inputs that contain duplicate abstract_problem_type values with schema_validation_failed (-32002) and error.data.reason=schema_invalid. Used at reduction_audit time to validate reduction_report.abstract_problem.

methods.name: campaign.status
methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status.

methods.name: campaign.topup
methods.summary: Monotonic budget top-up for an existing campaign (pilot-then-scale).
methods.description: Monotonic, additive budget top-up. Permitted when campaign status is running|paused|early_stopped|exhausted. If status is exhausted, successful topup MUST transition campaign status to running only if the campaign is no longer budget-exhausted after applying the top-up; otherwise it remains exhausted. If status is early_stopped (policy halt, not budget), topup adds budget but does NOT change the status; the caller must explicitly campaign.resume to re-enter running. If the campaign is completed, the engine MUST reject the request with campaign_not_active. campaign.topup MUST NOT return budget_exhausted; insufficient topups are represented by a successful result whose campaign_status remains exhausted. Idempotency note: on replay, the returned campaign_status/budget_snapshot reflects the first-call snapshot; callers MUST use campaign.status for current budget.

methods.name: campaign.pause
methods.description: Side-effecting. Permitted when campaign status is running|early_stopped|exhausted (running|early_stopped|exhausted → paused). Otherwise campaign_not_active.

methods.name: campaign.resume
methods.description: Side-effecting. Accepted when campaign status is paused|early_stopped. When resuming from paused or early_stopped, the engine MUST verify that no BudgetEnvelope dimension has remaining <= 0; if any does, it MUST reject with budget_exhausted (-32001) without changing state. Transitions paused|early_stopped → running. If status is exhausted, the engine MUST reject with budget_exhausted (-32001) without changing state (caller should campaign.topup first). If campaign is completed, returns campaign_not_active (-32015).

methods.name: campaign.complete
methods.summary: Mark a campaign as completed (→ completed).
methods.description: Side-effecting. Permitted when status is running|paused|early_stopped|exhausted|completed. Transitions the campaign into a terminal completed state. If already completed, the engine MUST treat this as a no-op (but still idempotent, with transition.changed=false). Read-only methods remain available after completion.

methods.name: node.get
methods.summary: Fetch a single IdeaNode by node_id within a campaign scope.
methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status.

methods.name: node.list

params.name: limit
params.description: Page size. Defaults to 50 if omitted. Maximum 500.

methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status. Results are always scoped to the given campaign; filters that do not match the campaign return empty results. If limit is omitted, the engine MUST treat it as 50.

methods.name: node.set_posterior
methods.summary: Record the externally computed belief-graph posterior for a node.

params.name: posterior

evidence_count.description: Number of independent observations in the belief graph backing this posterior (effective sample size).

gaia_package_ref.description: Optional URI of the belief-graph package (inside the external project) that produced this posterior.

params.description: The posterior to record. The engine stamps posterior.updated_at itself.

methods.description: Side-effecting. Writes the posterior computed by the external belief-graph tool onto the node, stamps posterior.updated_at, increments the node revision, and appends a set_posterior entry to the node mutation log. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active).

methods.name: node.set_lifecycle
methods.summary: Move a node between active, waiting_activation, and archived lifecycle states.

params.name: activation_condition
params.description: Required when lifecycle_state=waiting_activation (activation_condition_required otherwise); MUST be absent for other states (activation_condition_unexpected).

params.name: reason
params.description: Optional human-readable reason for the transition (recommended for archived). Recorded in the node mutation log.

methods.description: Side-effecting. Sets the node lifecycle state, increments the node revision, and appends a set_lifecycle entry (with the optional reason) to the node mutation log. waiting_activation MUST carry activation_condition; leaving waiting_activation clears the stored condition. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active).

methods.name: node.import_generated
methods.summary: Import one generation burst (a generation_pack_v1) as derived idea nodes.

params.name: pack
params.description: The burst to import. pack.campaign_id MUST equal the campaign_id param (mismatch is schema_validation_failed/pack_campaign_mismatch).

methods.description:
Side-effecting. The ONLY entry point for derived (non-seed) nodes. The engine: (1) validates the pack against generation_pack_v1 plus the semantic rules below; (2) refuses unless the campaign is running and the batch fits the nodes budget (budget_exhausted with details.exhausted_dimensions=["nodes"] when the batch would exceed max_nodes); (3) mints node_id/idea_id per candidate; (3b) rejects reserved engine-owned trace keys; (4) assembles each idea_card deterministically from card_fields (thesis_statement derived from rationale_draft by the same rule as seed import) and computes operator_trace.params.formalization engine-side (mode explain_then_formalize_deterministic_v1, source_artifact rationale_draft, rationale_hash over the engine's own sanitization) so node.promote can never drift; (5) validates every assembled node against idea_node_v1; (6) archives the pack verbatim (plus the engine-assembled nodes) as campaign artifact artifacts/generation/pack-<pack_id>.json; (7) writes nodes, one create node-log entry per node, and recomputed campaign usage (usage.nodes_used = total node count; steps are NOT consumed — import spends the nodes dimension only) under the campaign mutation lock with prepared->committed idempotency. Imported nodes are born with posterior=null and grounding_audit=null — evaluation authority stays with the belief layer; the pack carries no scores.

Semantic validation (all -32002 schema_validation_failed; error.data.reason values): pack_campaign_mismatch (pack.campaign_id != campaign_id param); operator_family_unknown (family outside the committed arity table below); operator_family_not_enabled (family in the table but outside the enabled set: LiteratureMining, FailureRouting — the remaining families are committed vocabulary rejected until their evidence disciplines land in this validator); operator_arity_invalid (parent_node_ids count violates the family's arity, duplicate parents, or AnalogyTransfer without a non-empty rationale_draft.analogy_mapping); trigger_not_enabled (trigger.kind outside the enabled set: manual, survey_updated, failure_recorded; the remaining vocabulary kinds are reserved and rejected until their seam is exercised; non-manual triggers additionally require trigger.artifact_ref); evidence_receipt_missing (an evidence URI in claims, rationale_draft.references, or a URI-shaped novelty_delta.closest_prior lacks a retrieval receipt in trace_inputs.retrieval_receipts [{uri, source}] or is absent from evidence_uris_used); placeholder_evidence_forbidden (the seed placeholder URI appears anywhere in a generated candidate — the scan walks every string in the candidate, including anchors, receipts, references, and novelty fields); evidence_snapshot_missing (a LiteratureMining candidate in a pack whose evidence_snapshot does not pin survey_artifact_ref + survey_content_hash); anchor_missing (LiteratureMining candidate without trace_inputs.anchor: kind=tension requires statement + non-empty ref_keys; FailureRouting with zero parents requires non-empty trace_inputs.failed_approach_refs, each pinned in evidence_snapshot.failed_approach_refs; FailureRouting with a parent requires that parent to be ARCHIVED — rerouting only makes sense around a node that died); gap_unanchored (LiteratureMining anchor.kind=gap without non-empty anchor.resolved_refs, each receipted); novelty_delta_non_novel (delta_type is parameter_tweak or rewording — declared non-novel by construction); parent_revisions_missing (a candidate's parent id is absent from evidence_snapshot.parent_revisions); parent_revision_invalid (a recorded read-time revision is not one the parent has actually had); intra_pack_duplicate (two candidates in one pack share the same normalized rationale draft — the engine's exact backstop behind the skill-side vector dedup, which must also compare within the burst); dedup_inconsistent (dedup.decision=unique with nearest_similarity >= 0.95, the fixed auto-drop bound); prompt_snapshot_missing (prompt provenance is MANDATORY: every candidate must declare provenance.prompt_snapshot_hash, a pack.prompt_snapshots entry's content must hash to it, every snapshot entry's content must match its own hash, and origin.prompt_hash must equal prompt_snapshot_hash — both hash the same rendered prompt, archived with the pack and verified at import); trace_key_reserved (trace_params carries a formalization key, or trace_inputs carries any of the six engine-owned keys: trigger, pack_artifact, parent_revisions, target_admission_route, dedup, novelty_delta). Parent nodes must exist in the campaign (node_not_found otherwise). dedup.decision=flagged without override_reason is rejected by the pack schema itself.

Arity table (operator_family -> required parent_node_ids count): Seed -> not importable here (seed import is campaign.init); LiteratureMining -> exactly 0; Mutation -> exactly 1; Recombination -> at least 2; AnalogyTransfer -> 0 or 1 (plus non-empty analogy_mapping); FailureRouting -> 0 or 1 (zero-parent form requires failed_approach_refs).

Crash recovery (import-specific; the generic delete-prepared-and-re-execute path is NOT used when any effect landed): the prepared idempotency record's result payload carries the minted ids, created_at, pack_artifact_ref, pack_hash, and archive_hash (sha256 over the ENTIRE archived artifact including the engine-assembled node payloads — recovery verifies it before completing any effect, so completion re-writes only content the original execution produced and validated). On retry, the engine probes all four effect classes — pack artifact present with matching pack_hash; every recorded node present with a value-equal IMMUTABLE projection (created_at, lineage, origin, operator_trace, rationale_draft — mutable fields may legitimately have moved); one create node-log entry per node; campaign usage consistent (nodes_used equals the store's node count). Missing effects are COMPLETED from the archived pack artifact — ids are never re-minted and content is never regenerated; only when NOTHING landed (no artifact, no recorded node) does the engine fall back to fresh execution. A value mismatch that cannot be completed refuses with internal_error/import_recovery_conflict for manual repair.

Additional semantics: the pack artifact filename is collision-guarded against artifacts already on disk (an id draw never overwrites an earlier burst's archived audit unit). Recovery completion deliberately does NOT re-check the nodes budget or campaign lifecycle: the burst's budget decision was made at prepare time, so completing an approved burst after an interleaved import may transiently overshoot max_nodes — the recomputed usage makes the overshoot visible and flips a running campaign to exhausted. A mutation attempted while another live process holds the campaign lock fails with internal_error/store_locked (holder pid + lock path in details); locks left by provably dead processes are reclaimed automatically.

methods.name: node.promote
methods.summary: Promote a node to a C2 handoff artifact (idea_card completeness + grounding + posterior gates) within a campaign scope.
methods.description: Side-effecting. Only permitted when campaign status is running. If exhausted: budget_exhausted. Otherwise campaign_not_active. Promotion gates (MUST): the IdeaCard must be schema-valid (including after placeholder evidence URIs are stripped), the formalization trace must be intact, grounding_audit.status must be pass, the node must be in the active lifecycle state (else promotion_blocked/node_not_active), and the node must carry a non-null posterior (else promotion_blocked/posterior_missing). No numeric posterior threshold is applied: review audits anchors, not scores. Optional method/formalism metadata inside IdeaCard is preserved as run-local context only and MUST NOT be treated as a registry gate. Conditional reduction gate (MUST): if the node has reduction_report != null, reduction_audit must be non-null and reduction_audit.status must be pass; otherwise reject with reduction_audit_failed (-32016) with error.data.reason=reduction_audit_missing or reduction_audit_not_pass.

methods.name: rank.compute
methods.summary: Order campaign nodes by their externally computed belief posterior.
methods.description: Side-effecting (writes a ranking artifact and consumes one step). Only permitted when campaign status is running; otherwise campaign_not_active. Filter is applied within campaign scope. Ranking semantics (MUST): nodes with lifecycle_state archived or waiting_activation, and nodes whose posterior is null, do not participate in the ordering; they MUST be reported explicitly in skipped_nodes with reason archived, waiting_activation, or no_posterior — never silently dropped. Remaining nodes are ordered by posterior.value descending; ties break by posterior.evidence_count descending, then by stable input order. An empty ranked_nodes list is a valid result (e.g., when no node has a posterior yet).
